---
title: "NHIS 2021 Data Analysis"
author: |
  Alyssa Bautista 
  Grant Pasamonte 
  Jamie Rosas Jr
  Group 7
output: word_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Load required packages
library(tidyverse)
library(psych)
```

## Script 1: Data Import

```{r data-import}
# Import the NHIS 2021 dataset
nhis <- read_csv("data/NHIS _Data_2021.csv")

# Explore the structure of the dataset
str(nhis)
summary(nhis)
head(nhis)
```

## Script 2: Data Cleaning

```{r script2-data-cleaning}
# Select only the required variables
nhis_selected <- nhis %>%
  select(
    AGEP_A,
    WEIGHTLBTC_A,
    HEIGHTTC_A,
    SEX_A,
    HISPALLP_A,
    EDUCP_A,
    PHSTAT_A,
    LSATIS4R_A
  )

# Recode missing and nonresponse values as NA
nhis_clean <- nhis_selected %>%
  mutate(
    AGEP_A = ifelse(AGEP_A %in% 97:99, NA, AGEP_A),
    WEIGHTLBTC_A = ifelse(WEIGHTLBTC_A %in% 996:999, NA, WEIGHTLBTC_A),
    HEIGHTTC_A = ifelse(HEIGHTTC_A %in% 96:99, NA, HEIGHTTC_A),
    SEX_A = ifelse(SEX_A %in% c(7, 9), NA, SEX_A),
    HISPALLP_A = ifelse(HISPALLP_A %in% 97:99, NA, HISPALLP_A),
    EDUCP_A = ifelse(EDUCP_A %in% 97:99, NA, EDUCP_A),
    PHSTAT_A = ifelse(PHSTAT_A %in% 7:9, NA, PHSTAT_A),
    LSATIS4R_A = ifelse(LSATIS4R_A %in% 7:9, NA, LSATIS4R_A)
  ) %>%
  drop_na()

# Recode EDUCP_A into four categories
nhis_clean <- nhis_clean %>%
  mutate(
    EDUCP_A = case_when(
      EDUCP_A %in% 0:3 ~ "Less than High School",
      EDUCP_A == 4 ~ "High School Graduate",
      EDUCP_A %in% 5:7 ~ "Some College Education",
      EDUCP_A %in% 8:10 ~ "College Graduate or Better"
    ),
    EDUCP_A = factor(
      EDUCP_A,
      levels = c(
        "Less than High School",
        "High School Graduate",
        "Some College Education",
        "College Graduate or Better"
      )
    )
  )

# Save the cleaned dataset
write_csv(nhis_clean, "data/nhis_clean.csv")

# Check the cleaned data
str(nhis_clean)
summary(nhis_clean)
head(nhis_clean)
```

## Script 3: Univariate Analysis

```{r script3-summary-stats}
# Summary statistics for quantitative variables
summary(nhis_clean$AGEP_A)
mean(nhis_clean$AGEP_A)
median(nhis_clean$AGEP_A)
sd(nhis_clean$AGEP_A)

summary(nhis_clean$WEIGHTLBTC_A)
mean(nhis_clean$WEIGHTLBTC_A)
median(nhis_clean$WEIGHTLBTC_A)
sd(nhis_clean$WEIGHTLBTC_A)

summary(nhis_clean$HEIGHTTC_A)
mean(nhis_clean$HEIGHTTC_A)
median(nhis_clean$HEIGHTTC_A)
sd(nhis_clean$HEIGHTTC_A)
```

```{r script3-age-plots}
# Base R histogram of age
hist(
  nhis_clean$AGEP_A,
  main = "Distribution of Age",
  xlab = "Age in Years",
  ylab = "Count",
  col = "lightgreen"
)

# ggplot2 histogram of age
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = AGEP_A)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, fill = "green4", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Age", x = "Age in Years", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()

# Base R boxplot of age
boxplot(
  nhis_clean$AGEP_A,
  main = "Boxplot of Age",
  ylab = "Age in Years",
  col = "yellow"
)

# ggplot2 boxplot of age
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(y = AGEP_A)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "gold") +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Age", y = "Age in Years") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r script3-weight-plots}
# Base R histogram of weight
hist(
  nhis_clean$WEIGHTLBTC_A,
  main = "Distribution of Weight",
  xlab = "Weight in Pounds",
  ylab = "Count",
  col = "orangered"
)

# ggplot2 histogram of weight
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = WEIGHTLBTC_A)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "orangered3", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Weight", x = "Weight in Pounds", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()

# Base R boxplot of weight
boxplot(
  nhis_clean$WEIGHTLBTC_A,
  main = "Boxplot of Weight",
  ylab = "Weight in Pounds",
  col = "rosybrown"
)

# ggplot2 boxplot of weight
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(y = WEIGHTLBTC_A)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightpink4") +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Weight", y = "Weight in Pounds") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r script3-height-plots}
# Base R histogram of height
hist(
  nhis_clean$HEIGHTTC_A,
  main = "Distribution of Height",
  xlab = "Height in Inches",
  ylab = "Count",
  col = "dodgerblue"
)

# ggplot2 histogram of height
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = HEIGHTTC_A)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "dodgerblue4", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Height", x = "Height in Inches", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()

# Base R boxplot of height
boxplot(
  nhis_clean$HEIGHTTC_A,
  main = "Boxplot of Height",
  ylab = "Height in Inches",
  col = "mediumslateblue"
)

# ggplot2 boxplot of height
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(y = HEIGHTTC_A)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "slateblue4") +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Height", y = "Height in Inches") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r script3-frequency-tables}
# Frequency tables for qualitative variables
table(nhis_clean$SEX_A)
table(nhis_clean$HISPALLP_A)
table(nhis_clean$EDUCP_A)
table(nhis_clean$PHSTAT_A)
table(nhis_clean$LSATIS4R_A)
```

```{r script3-sex-barplots}
# Base R barplot for sex
barplot(
  table(nhis_clean$SEX_A),
  names.arg = c("Male", "Female"),
  xlab = "Sex",
  ylab = "Count",
  col = topo.colors(2),
  main = "Bar Chart of Sex",
  border = "black"
)

# ggplot2 barplot for sex
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = factor(SEX_A, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Male", "Female")))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Bar Chart of Sex", x = "Sex", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r script3-race-barplots}
# Base R barplot for race and ethnicity
barplot(
  table(nhis_clean$HISPALLP_A),
  names.arg = c(
    "Hispanic",
    "NH White",
    "NH Black",
    "NH Asian",
    "NH AIAN",
    "NH AIAN + Other",
    "Other/Multiple"
  ),
  cex.names = 0.7,
  xlab = "Race/Ethnicity",
  ylab = "Count",
  col = rainbow(7),
  main = "Bar Chart of Race/Ethnicity",
  border = "black"
)

# ggplot2 barplot for race and ethnicity
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = factor(
  HISPALLP_A,
  levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7),
  labels = c(
    "Hispanic",
    "NH White",
    "NH Black",
    "NH Asian",
    "NH AIAN",
    "NH AIAN + Other",
    "Other/Multiple"
  )
))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Bar Chart of Race/Ethnicity", x = "Race/Ethnicity", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8))
```

```{r script3-educ-barplots}
# Base R barplot for education
barplot(
  table(nhis_clean$EDUCP_A),
  xlab = "Educational Level",
  ylab = "Count",
  col = heat.colors(4),
  main = "Bar Chart of Educational Level",
  border = "black"
)

# ggplot2 barplot for education
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = EDUCP_A)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Bar Chart of Educational Level", x = "Educational Level", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8))
```

```{r script3-health-barplots}
# Base R barplot for general health status
barplot(
  table(nhis_clean$PHSTAT_A),
  names.arg = c("Excellent", "Very good", "Good", "Fair", "Poor"),
  xlab = "General Health Status",
  ylab = "Count",
  col = terrain.colors(5),
  main = "Bar Chart of General Health Status",
  border = "black"
)

# ggplot2 barplot for general health status
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = factor(
  PHSTAT_A,
  levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5),
  labels = c("Excellent", "Very good", "Good", "Fair", "Poor")
))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Bar Chart of General Health Status", x = "General Health Status", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r script3-life-barplots}
# Base R barplot for life satisfaction
barplot(
  table(nhis_clean$LSATIS4R_A),
  names.arg = c("Very Satisfied", "Satisfied", "Dissatisfied", "Very Dissatisfied"),
  xlab = "Life Satisfaction",
  ylab = "Count",
  col = cm.colors(4),
  main = "Bar Chart of Life Satisfaction",
  border = "black"
)

# ggplot2 barplot for life satisfaction
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = factor(
  LSATIS4R_A,
  levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  labels = c("Very Satisfied", "Satisfied", "Dissatisfied", "Very Dissatisfied")
))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Bar Chart of Life Satisfaction", x = "Life Satisfaction", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()
```

## Script 4: Bivariate Analysis

```{r script4-age-sex-boxplot}
# Base R boxplot of age by sex
boxplot(
  AGEP_A ~ factor(SEX_A, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Male", "Female")),
  data = nhis_clean,
  main = "Distribution of Age by Sex",
  xlab = "Sex",
  ylab = "Age",
  col = c("mediumseagreen", "mediumslateblue")
)

# ggplot2 boxplot of age by sex
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(
  x = factor(SEX_A, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Male", "Female")),
  y = AGEP_A
)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Age by Sex", x = "Sex", y = "Age") +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r script4-age-educ-boxplot}
# Base R boxplot of age by education level
boxplot(
  AGEP_A ~ EDUCP_A,
  data = nhis_clean,
  main = "Distribution of Age by Education Level",
  xlab = "Education Level",
  ylab = "Age",
  col = "lightblue",
  las = 1,
  cex.axis = 0.8
)

# ggplot2 boxplot of age by education level and sex
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(
  x = EDUCP_A,
  y = AGEP_A,
  fill = factor(SEX_A, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Male", "Female"))
)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Age by Sex and Education") +
  xlab("Education Level") +
  ylab("Age") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("mediumseagreen", "mediumslateblue"), name = "Sex") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r script4-clustered-bar}
# Clustered bar chart of general health status and life satisfaction
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(
  x = factor(
    PHSTAT_A,
    levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5),
    labels = c("Excellent", "Very good", "Good", "Fair", "Poor")
  ),
  fill = factor(
    LSATIS4R_A,
    levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
    labels = c("Very Satisfied", "Satisfied", "Dissatisfied", "Very Dissatisfied")
  )
)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Life Satisfaction by General Health Status",
    x = "General Health Status",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Life Satisfaction"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r script4-scatter-correlation}
# Base R scatterplot of height and weight
plot(
  nhis_clean$HEIGHTTC_A,
  nhis_clean$WEIGHTLBTC_A,
  pch = 19,
  col = "cyan4",
  main = "Scatterplot of Height vs. Weight",
  xlab = "Height in Inches",
  ylab = "Weight in Pounds"
)

# ggplot2 scatterplot of height and weight
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = HEIGHTTC_A, y = WEIGHTLBTC_A)) +
  geom_point(color = "cyan4") +
  ggtitle("Scatterplot of Height vs. Weight") +
  xlab("Height in Inches") +
  ylab("Weight in Pounds") +
  theme_minimal()

# Correlation coefficient
cor(nhis_clean$HEIGHTTC_A, nhis_clean$WEIGHTLBTC_A)
```

## Script 5: Multivariate Analysis

```{r script5-scatter-phstat}
# Enhanced scatterplot colored by sex and faceted by general health status
scatter_phstat <- ggplot(
  nhis_clean,
  aes(
    x = HEIGHTTC_A,
    y = WEIGHTLBTC_A,
    color = factor(SEX_A, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Male", "Female"))
  )
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ PHSTAT_A) +
  labs(
    title = "Weight vs. Height by Sex, Faceted by General Health Status",
    x = "Height (inches)",
    y = "Weight (pounds)",
    color = "Sex"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

scatter_phstat
```

```{r script5-scatter-educ}
# Enhanced scatterplot colored by sex and faceted by education level
scatter_educp <- ggplot(
  nhis_clean,
  aes(
    x = HEIGHTTC_A,
    y = WEIGHTLBTC_A,
    color = factor(SEX_A, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Male", "Female"))
  )
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ EDUCP_A) +
  labs(
    title = "Weight vs. Height by Sex, Faceted by Education Level",
    x = "Height (inches)",
    y = "Weight (pounds)",
    color = "Sex"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

scatter_educp
```

```{r script5-save-plots}
# Save multivariate scatterplots to the output folder
ggsave("output/scatter_height_weight_by_sex_phstat.png", scatter_phstat,
       width = 11, height = 7, dpi = 300)

ggsave("output/scatter_height_weight_by_sex_educp.png", scatter_educp,
       width = 11, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```

```{r script5-pairs-panels}
# Create a correlation scatterplot matrix for age, weight, and height
corr_data <- nhis_clean %>%
  select(AGEP_A, WEIGHTLBTC_A, HEIGHTTC_A) %>%
  drop_na()

pairs.panels(
  corr_data,
  method = "pearson",
  hist.col = "lightblue",
  density = TRUE,
  ellipses = TRUE,
  lm = TRUE,
  main = "Correlation Scatter Plot Matrix: Age, Weight, and Height"
)
```

```{r script5-save-pairs}
# Save the correlation scatterplot matrix to the output folder
png("output/pairs_panels_age_weight_height.png", width = 1200, height = 1200, res = 150)

pairs.panels(
  corr_data,
  method = "pearson",
  hist.col = "lightblue",
  density = TRUE,
  ellipses = TRUE,
  lm = TRUE,
  main = "Correlation Scatter Plot Matrix: Age, Weight, and Height"
)

dev.off()
```
